seoTitle: Add a Button in HubSpot: Create & Link a CTA
metaDescription: Create a custom call-to-action button in HubSpot, link it to a URL, style its colors and padding, and track clicks with the CTA analytics engine.
intro: A well-placed button can make the difference in whether a visitor takes action, and HubSpot's Button module gives you control over both the design and the destination link. This guide covers dragging the module into your page or email, setting the display text and target URL, and adjusting visual styles like color and padding. It ends with checking HubSpot's analytics to measure how the button performs.

steps.heading: Open your content editor
steps.body: Select the target page or email layout where you want to add the button.

steps.heading: Find the Button module
steps.body: Look in the left sidebar module tray for the core Button component.

steps.heading: Drop it into your layout
steps.body: Drag and drop the Button module into your content canvas.

steps.heading: Set the text and link
steps.body: Edit the display text and configure the target URL, meeting link, or file the button should point to.

steps.heading: Style the button
steps.body: Adjust background color, hover states, border radius, and padding to match your design.

steps.heading: Align and test
steps.body: Set the alignment to center, left, or full width, then test the click redirection before publishing.

faq.question: Can I link a HubSpot button to an external URL?
faq.answer: Yes, the button's linking properties support routing to external URLs, meeting links, or uploaded files.

faq.question: How do I track clicks on a CTA button?
faq.answer: HubSpot's CTA analytics engine lets you measure clicks and engagement once the button is published.

faq.question: Can I change the button's color and shape?
faq.answer: Yes, the design panel lets you adjust background fill, border radius, padding, and text alignment.

faq.question: Does the button module work in both pages and emails?
faq.answer: Yes, the same Button or CTA module is available in both the page editor and the email editor.
